title: RAND Army Research Division
description: News from the RAND Arroyo Center, the U.S. Army's only federally funded research and development center. RAND Arroyo Center conducts objective analytic research on major policy concerns, with an emphasis on mid- to long-term policy issues, helps the Army improve effectiveness and efficiency, and provides short-term assistance on urgent problems.
text:
Understanding the "Will to Fight"
Will to fight helps determine whether a military unit — or a nation — stays in the fight and also how well it fights. Researchers explored this phenomenon to better understand and influence the will to fight at both the national level and at the tactical and operational levels.
U.S. Army Roles in the Indo Pacific
RAND researchers designed three scenarios to examine the Army's role in the Indo-Pacific out to 2035. Their findings are incorporated into a new report that describes potential trends, enduring constraints, and Indo-Pacific ally and partner dynamics that will shape where, when, and how the Joint Force might operate within the theater.
Future Wars Program
The Future Wars program serves as the Army's intellectual vanguard, exploring how wars will be fought, what capabilities will be required, and how operational concepts must evolve to ensure battlefield dominance against peer and near-peer adversaries.
